description: Sandbox Redesign — Flow-based workflow visualization engine replacing the old Phase 45 sandbox. Visual n8n-style canvas with custom nodes, node editing, live execution overlay, and production monitoring. See docs/plans/SANDBOX_REDESIGN_EXECUTION_PLAN.md for full architecture.

userStories.title: Workflow Graph Type System + Node Metadata Registry
userStories.description: Create lib/workflow-graph/ with core TypeScript types representing an n8n workflow as a renderable graph. Define WorkflowGraph (nodes + edges + metadata), GraphNode (id, name, type, category, position, parameters, editableParams), GraphEdge (source, target, sourceHandle, targetHandle), NodeCategory enum (trigger, ai_llm, email_send, data_db, logic_routing, tracking, utility, unknown), and EditableParamDescriptor (key, label, type, path, options). Create NODE_TYPE_REGISTRY mapping ~20 observed n8n node types (scheduleTrigger, webhook, gmailTrigger, gmail, httpRequest, postgres, googleSheets, code, set, if, splitInBatches, merge, limit, wait, respondToWebhook, html) to their NodeCategory, display label, and color. httpRequest nodes need contextual classification helper (ai_llm if URL contains openai/anthropic, tracking if URL contains event tracking, email_send if SMTP, else utility). Export everything from lib/workflow-graph/index.ts barrel.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/workflow-graph/types.ts exports WorkflowGraph, GraphNode, GraphEdge, NodeCategory, EditableParamDescriptor, WorkflowMetadata types
NodeCategory is a string union or enum with exactly: trigger, ai_llm, email_send, data_db, logic_routing, tracking, utility, unknown
GraphNode has fields: id, name, type, category (NodeCategory), position ({x,y}), parameters (Record<string,unknown>), editableParams (EditableParamDescriptor[]), credentials (string[])
WorkflowMetadata has fields: workflowId, workflowName, workflowType (WorkflowType from phase45 types), active (boolean), nodeCount, edgeCount
lib/workflow-graph/registry.ts exports NODE_TYPE_REGISTRY (Record<string, {category, label, color}>)
Registry covers all 16+ observed n8n node types from base templates
classifyHttpRequestNode() function exported that takes node parameters and returns NodeCategory based on URL/credential analysis
lib/workflow-graph/index.ts re-exports all public types and registry
npx tsc --noEmit passes (use NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=4096')
userStories.notes: Committed at 84a088b. Types: WorkflowGraph, GraphNode, GraphEdge, NodeCategory (8 values), EditableParamDescriptor, WorkflowMetadata. Registry: 16 n8n node types + classifyHttpRequestNode contextual classifier.

userStories.title: n8n-to-Graph Adapter + dagre Auto-Layout
userStories.description: Create lib/workflow-graph/adapter.ts with a pure function transformN8nToGraph(n8nWorkflow: unknown): WorkflowGraph. It must: parse n8n JSON (nodes[], connections{}), filter out sticky notes (n8n-nodes-internal.stickyNote), map each n8n node to a GraphNode using the registry + classifyHttpRequestNode, parse the connections object into GraphEdge[], detect editable params per node using known patterns (AI prompt nodes get 'prompt' param, schedule nodes get 'rule.interval' param, limit nodes get 'maxItems'), compute metadata. Create lib/workflow-graph/layout.ts with autoLayout(graph: WorkflowGraph): WorkflowGraph that uses dagre to compute left-to-right positions for all nodes (rankdir='LR', nodesep=60, ranksep=120, node width=200, height=80). The adapter should call autoLayout if nodes lack positions or all positions are 0,0.
acceptanceCriteria: lib/workflow-graph/adapter.ts exports transformN8nToGraph function
Function correctly parses n8n workflow JSON nodes array into GraphNode[]
Function correctly parses n8n connections object into GraphEdge[]
Sticky notes (n8n-nodes-internal.stickyNote) are filtered out
Each node gets correct NodeCategory from registry + httpRequest classifier
Editable params detected for AI prompt nodes, schedule trigger nodes, limit nodes
lib/workflow-graph/layout.ts exports autoLayout function using dagre
Auto-layout produces left-to-right graph with no overlapping nodes
dagre and @types/dagre are installed in package.json
npx tsc --noEmit passes (use NODE_OPTIONS='--max-old-space-size=4096')
userStories.notes: Committed at 43ccd52. transformN8nToGraph adapter parses nodes/connections, filters sticky notes, classifies httpRequest, detects editable params. autoLayout via dagre (LR, nodesep=60, ranksep=120). dagre + @types/dagre installed.

userStories.title: SMTP Endpoint Authentication (localhost + secret)
userStories.description: The sidecar /send and /check-reply HTTP endpoints are intentionally unauthenticated (n8n workflows call them directly). This is a P0 security gap: any process reachable on the droplet's port can send arbitrary emails. Fix: (1) Add requireSmtpCaller middleware that rejects requests not originating from localhost (127.0.0.1/::1/::ffff:127.0.0.1); (2) if SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is set, also enforce X-Smtp-Secret header match for defence-in-depth. Add smtpWebhookSecret to SidecarConfig and wire from env. Existing n8n workflow HTTP nodes already call localhost so no template changes required for the IP guard.
acceptanceCriteria: SidecarConfig has smtpWebhookSecret?: string field
requireSmtpCaller middleware rejects non-localhost remote addresses with 403
requireSmtpCaller also rejects when X-Smtp-Secret header is wrong (if SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET is set)
requireSmtpCaller allows localhost callers with correct/missing secret when SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET not set
/send route uses requireSmtpCaller middleware
/check-reply route uses requireSmtpCaller middleware
Main entry point loads smtpWebhookSecret from process.env.SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET
cd sidecar && npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: SEC-001: requireSmtpCaller middleware added to /send and /check-reply. Localhost IP guard (127.0.0.1/::1/::ffff:127.0.0.1) applied unconditionally. If SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET env var is set, X-Smtp-Secret header is also required. smtpWebhookSecret added to SidecarConfig. Main entry wires process.env.SMTP_WEBHOOK_SECRET. tsc --noEmit passes.

userStories.title: Sidecar Token Disk Persistence
userStories.description: After performHandshake() the sidecar token is stored only in memory. Process restarts (n8n crash, OOM kill, deploy) lose the token and break heartbeats until the droplet is re-provisioned. Fix: after acquiring the token, write it to $SIDECAR_DATA_DIR/.sidecar_token (default /app/data). On start(), load from disk before calling performHandshake(); if a valid token is found set this.config.sidecarToken so the handshake is skipped. Handle file errors gracefully (log + continue) so a missing/corrupt file falls back to re-handshake.
acceptanceCriteria: persistToken(token) writes token to TOKEN_FILE path with mkdirSync for parent dir
loadPersistedToken() reads TOKEN_FILE and returns token string or null on any error
start() calls loadPersistedToken() before bootstrapN8n() and applies result to this.config.sidecarToken
performHandshake() sets token then immediately calls persistToken()
TODO comment removed from performHandshake()
SIDECAR_DATA_DIR env var controls the data directory (default /app/data)
cd sidecar && npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: SEC-002: TOKEN_FILE constant = $SIDECAR_DATA_DIR/.sidecar_token (default /app/data). persistToken() writes with mode 0o600 + mkdirSync. loadPersistedToken() reads on start() before bootstrapN8n(); applies token to config so performHandshake() skips re-handshake. performHandshake() calls persistToken() after receiving token. TODO comment removed. tsc --noEmit passes.

userStories.title: Command Injection Guard — docker-manager.ts
userStories.description: Sanitize all shell-interpolated arguments in docker-manager.ts to prevent OS command injection via exec/execAsync. Add sanitizeArg() with regex allowlists for image names, tags, numeric values, and timestamp patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: sanitizeArg(value, pattern, label) validates input against regex allowlist before shell interpolation
DOCKER_IMAGE_RE /^[a-zA-Z0-9._\-\/]+$/ guards image names in pullImage()
DOCKER_TAG_RE /^[a-zA-Z0-9._\-]+$/ guards tags in pullImage() and swapContainer()
NUMERIC_RE /^[0-9]+$/ guards lines param in getLogs()
SINCE_RE /^[0-9TZ:.\-]+$/ guards since param in getLogs()
cd sidecar && npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: Added sanitizeArg + 4 regex constants. Applied to pullImage (imageName+tag), swapContainer (newImageTag), getLogs (lines+since). All 3 Snyk HIGH command injection flows sanitized.

userStories.title: SSRF Guard on Admin Routes
userStories.description: Add private IP blocking to admin routes that make outbound requests to sidecar URLs from the database. Defense-in-depth against DB-record poisoning.
acceptanceCriteria: heartbeat/route.ts rejects sidecar URLs pointing to RFC-1918/localhost/link-local IPs
sidecar-command/route.ts rejects sidecar hosts matching private IP patterns
freeze-workspace/route.ts confirmed as false positive (no outbound HTTP)
Blocked requests return clear error messages
npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: Added IP validation to heartbeat and sidecar-command routes. Blocks 127.0.0.1, ::1, 10.x, 192.168.x, 172.16-31.x, 0.0.0.0, 169.254.x. freeze-workspace is a false positive.

userStories.title: ReDoS Prevention in Credential Map
userStories.description: Replace new RegExp(placeholder, 'g') with replaceAll() in applyCredentialMap to prevent ReDoS from unsanitized regex input.
acceptanceCriteria: workflowStr.replace(new RegExp(...)) changed to workflowStr.replaceAll(placeholder, replacement)
Literal string replacement — no dynamic regex construction
cd sidecar && npx tsc --noEmit passes
userStories.notes: replaceAll is available in ES2022 (sidecar target). Eliminates both ReDoS and regex injection vectors.
